title: Hormuz disruption decision clock
disclaimer: This is a deterministic, offline local demonstration linked to the simulated Hormuz replay. Every number is explicitly labelled Simulated, Seeded, or User-entered. It does not use current inventory, live laycan, terminal, tanker, approval, market, or procurement data and it must not be used as an operational instruction.

metrics.label: Baseline decision window
metrics.rationale: The local clock derives this window from the seeded laycan cutoff less the simulated baseline approval delay.

metrics.label: Seeded stock-cover threshold
metrics.rationale: A fixed nine-day local stock-cover baseline is used solely to demonstrate the time-pressure calculation.

metrics.label: Baseline approval delay
metrics.rationale: The default is a visible prototype assumption, not a measurement of a real approval workflow.

metrics.label: Seeded laycan cutoff
metrics.rationale: The local fixture fixes a four-day booking cutoff to make timing trade-offs testable; it is not a current laycan.

metrics.label: Protected inventory buffer
metrics.rationale: The local scenario reserves two days of the seeded cover as a protected operating buffer before escalation.

metrics.label: Usable stock cover
metrics.rationale: The local clock subtracts the simulated protected buffer from the seeded stock-cover threshold.

metrics.label: Effective decision lead time
metrics.rationale: With the default approval assumption, the analyst has this much time after the replay signal to submit a decision.

metrics.label: Last responsible action
metrics.rationale: A decision submitted after this simulated offset cannot complete the assumed approval before the seeded laycan cutoff.

metrics.label: Effective approval delay
metrics.rationale: This default is changeable through the local demo endpoint and is never presented as an observed approval duration.

stages.label: Replay signal received
stages.detail: The clock starts from the simulated Hormuz replay signal, not from a live maritime or geopolitical feed.

stages.label: Analyst assumptions reviewed
stages.action: Confirm scenario assumptions and evidence limits
stages.detail: A sample review checkpoint is retained as a user-entered process assumption; it is not a recorded real analyst action.

stages.label: Assumed approval completion
stages.detail: This is the simulated default approval duration applied only to calculate the local decision deadline.

stages.label: Last responsible action
stages.action: Submit the decision before the approval buffer is consumed
stages.detail: This simulated deadline equals the seeded laycan cutoff minus the assumed approval delay; no booking is created.

stages.label: Seeded laycan cutoff
stages.action: Escalate if no analyst-approved contingency exists
stages.detail: A local, fixed booking cutoff makes the demonstration testable and must not be interpreted as a current commercial laycan.

stages.label: Seeded stock-cover threshold
stages.action: Reassess resilient supply and inventory assumptions
stages.detail: This fixed nine-day threshold is not a real inventory reading or a claim about national or refinery stock levels.

assumptions.label: Seeded stock cover
assumptions.rationale: A fixed prototype inventory baseline is used for the clock; no live refinery, strategic reserve, or terminal inventory is connected.

assumptions.label: Protected operating buffer
assumptions.rationale: The scenario reserves this portion of seeded cover before treating the remaining time as usable decision capacity.

assumptions.label: Laycan booking cutoff
assumptions.rationale: This is a local scheduling baseline for a replay, not a live cargo offer, berth slot, or booking window.

assumptions.label: Approval delay
assumptions.rationale: The default approval duration is explicitly assumed; callers may replace it with a user-entered demo value.

assumptions.label: Last responsible action formula
assumptions.rationale: The deterministic formula prevents the interface from implying that an AI or live operations feed has calculated the deadline.
